GlobalPolicy.module-level-focus: Fast deterministic checks per module with trace assertions
GlobalPolicy.wave-level-focus: Integration checks for merged surfaces touched by current wave only
GlobalPolicy.phase-level-focus: Full regression + traceability + fixture corpus validation
GlobalPolicy.redaction: Never log model API keys, user document content, or raw OOXML payloads exceeding 10KB. Edit-pipeline diff snippets (M-EDIT.render_diff: before_snippet, after_snippet, anchor.value) and extracted text fed to the LLM (M-EDIT.extract_text_with_anchors output) carry user content and are restricted to DEBUG log level only; INFO and above must reference them by op_id and length, never by literal text. Backup file paths may be logged at INFO; original document paths beyond the project workspace must be logged as basename only.

evidence-types.type-1: Exact assert on return value, state, or file content hash
evidence-types.type-2: Required log markers appeared in correct order; forbidden markers absent
evidence-types.type-3: End-to-end file produced + passes validation pipeline
evidence-types.type-4: Bounded evaluation against rubric (only where exact assert impossible)

TestFixtures.fixture-1: Well-formed DOCX passing all D-H rules
TestFixtures.fixture-5: Well-formed PPTX passing all P-H rules
TestFixtures.fixture-7: Minimal docx-js code producing valid DOCX
TestFixtures.fixture-8: Minimal pptxgenjs code producing valid PPTX
TestFixtures.fixture-9: Code with require('fs') for sandbox violation test
TestFixtures.fixture-10: Code with require('net') for sandbox violation test
TestFixtures.fixture-13: DOCX with GRACE Custom XML Parts already injected
TestFixtures.fixture-14: DOCX with non-GRACE Custom XML Parts for preservation test
TestFixtures.fixture-15: DOCX containing existing w:ins and w:del entries for accept_change / reject_change tests
TestFixtures.fixture-16: DOCX containing top-level comments in word/comments.xml plus matching commentRange markers in document.xml
TestFixtures.fixture-17: DOCX containing parent comment plus reply linked through word/commentsExtended.xml (w15:commentEx paraIdParent)
TestFixtures.fixture-18: DOCX containing ASCII straight quotes inside w:t for escape_smart_quotes round-trip tests
TestFixtures.fixture-19: DOCX containing w:durableId >= 0x7FFFFFFF for pack auto-repair test
TestFixtures.fixture-20: DOCX whose word/_rels/document.xml.rels references a missing Target for OOXML_RELATIONSHIP_BROKEN test
TestFixtures.fixture-21: ~5MB DOCX, ~100 paragraphs distributed across ~100 pages, used for the M-EDIT non-functional latency budget (Wave-5-2 evidence-7). Generated procedurally by tests/fixtures/generate_large.py rather than checked in if > LFS threshold; see fixture-generation note below.
TestFixtures.fixture-note-1: fixture for V-M-EDIT scenario-24 (hash collision) is NOT pre-built on disk: the test stubs mint.edit._paragraph_hash via monkeypatch on a freshly-built two-paragraph fixture, so brute-forcing a real 8-hex SHA1 collision is unnecessary.
TestFixtures.fixture-note-2: fixture-21 (large_5mb) MAY be regenerated locally by the test runner (deterministic seed) to keep the repository under git-LFS thresholds; the generator script is the source of truth and is itself version-controlled.

VF-001.NAME: CodeGenerationPipeline
VF-001.scenario: Frontier/medium model writes JS code → sandbox executes → file produced
VF-001.expected-outcome: Valid DOCX/PPTX file passes L1 validation, no P0 errors

invariants.inv-1: Sandbox returns within 30s or raises SANDBOX_TIMEOUT
invariants.inv-2: Output file is well-formed OOXML (zip + XML parse)
invariants.inv-3: No forbidden patterns in executed code

VF-002.NAME: TemplateFillPipeline
VF-002.scenario: Small model provides JSON → template engine fills master → file produced
VF-002.expected-outcome: Valid DOCX/PPTX from template, design tokens applied, no P0 errors

invariants.inv-1: Template output is valid OOXML ZIP

VF-003.NAME: ValidateAndFix
VF-003.scenario: Document with violations → validation detects → auto-fix applies safe/visual fixes
VF-003.expected-outcome: Safe fixes silent, visual fixes with diff, destructive rejected with hint

invariants.inv-1: Backup file exists before any fix
invariants.inv-4: Cascade detected if violations not converging

VF-004.NAME: StyleExtraction
VF-004.expected-outcome: JSON contains colors, typography, layout; Layer 1 = 100% accurate

invariants.inv-1: Layer 1 output exactly matches theme/styles.xml content
invariants.inv-2: Output JSON is valid against design-tokens schema

VF-005.NAME: VisualQA
VF-005.scenario: Document → L1 validation + L2 structural ZIP analysis
VF-005.expected-outcome: QA report with confidence scores; malformed or incomplete OOXML structure is surfaced deterministically

invariants.inv-3: Current executable L2 evidence is structural, not rendered visual overlap; render-based Gotenberg checks require a future VF before swarm agents may rely on them

VF-006.NAME: GRACEInjection
VF-006.scenario: Document → GRACE manifest + instructions injected as Custom XML Parts
VF-006.expected-outcome: Metadata readable via OOXML parsers; existing Custom XML preserved

invariants.inv-2: Non-GRACE Custom XML Parts byte-identical after injection

VF-007.NAME: ModularGenerationPipeline
VF-007.scenario: Complex user prompt → M-PLAN decomposes into sections → M-SECTION generates code per section → M-ASSEMBLE builds complete document
VF-007.expected-outcome: DOCX with all planned sections, valid structure, passes L1 validation, no Word repair required

invariants.inv-1: Plan has at least 1 section with valid type and description
invariants.inv-2: Each section code has no import/require, no fs references, bracket balance OK
invariants.inv-3: Assembly JS is valid and produces output file within 30s sandbox timeout
invariants.inv-4: Output file is well-formed OOXML (ZIP + XML parse)
invariants.inv-5: Failed sections retried up to max_retries before overall failure
invariants.inv-6: Postprocessor applied: no empty comments.xml, tblLayout fixed, styles deduped

required-signals.trace-sequence: BLOCK_PLAN_DECOMPOSE -> BLOCK_PLAN_VALIDATE -> BLOCK_SECTION_GENERATE (per section) -> BLOCK_ASSEMBLE_TEMPLATE -> BLOCK_ASSEMBLE_EXECUTE -> BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS -> RETURNED

forbidden-signals.forbidden-1: [Section] import or require in generated code

VF-008.NAME: PlanValidation
VF-008.scenario: LLM produces plan JSON → M-PLAN validates structure → rejects invalid plans with actionable error
VF-008.expected-outcome: Valid plans pass; invalid plans (empty sections, bad types, missing fields) rejected with clear error

invariants.inv-1: Plan has format matching request (docx or pptx)
invariants.inv-2: Each section has id, type (from enum), and description
invariants.inv-3: Style IDs are standard (Heading1-6, ListParagraph, Hyperlink)
invariants.inv-4: Page setup dimensions are valid DXA (width > 0, height > 0)

VF-009.NAME: SectionRetryRecovery
VF-009.scenario: Section generation fails (syntax error, truncation) → M-SECTION retries with refined prompt → succeeds on retry. If all retries exhausted → placeholder generated, assembly proceeds.
VF-009.expected-outcome: Individual section failure produces placeholder (title + italic error message). Assembly includes placeholders. Overall result success=False but document still produced.

invariants.inv-2: Retry prompt includes: original section spec + previous error message + previous failed code as negative example
invariants.inv-3: Global retry budget enforced: max_total_retries (default 20) across all sections
invariants.inv-4: If all retries exhausted for a section, placeholder code generated (not empty, not skipped)
invariants.inv-5: Assembly includes all sections — failed ones have placeholder children
invariants.inv-6: CreateResult.success=False if any section used placeholder, sections_succeeded < sections_total

VF-010.NAME: DocumentEditPipeline
VF-010.scenario: EditPlan JSON (typed ops) → M-EDIT validates plan → M-OOXML unpacks → M-EDIT resolves anchors against original tree → applies typed handlers in order → M-OOXML packs → M-VALIDATE checks output
VF-010.expected-outcome: Output passes M-VALIDATE in the configured severity mode; backup of the original is preserved on disk; per-op diff is returned with before/after snippets; non-edited binary parts and unrelated XML parts round-trip unchanged; the LLM (when used to produce the plan) never sees raw OOXML XML

invariants.inv-1: Every op_id in the plan is unique
invariants.inv-2: Every anchor.value is non-empty, length ≤ 512, no ASCII control characters
invariants.inv-3: Backup file is created before any tree mutation
invariants.inv-4: Anchors of every type (paragraph_index, hash, text) resolve against the ORIGINAL tree captured at unpack; no re-indexing across ops
invariants.inv-5: Tracked-change ops emit ECMA-376 w:ins/w:del with author and date; w:rPr is preserved on both branches
invariants.inv-6: Comment replies are linked through word/commentsExtended.xml (w15:commentEx paraIdParent), never by nesting commentRange in document.xml
invariants.inv-7: commentRangeStart/End markers are siblings of w:r, never nested inside w:r
invariants.inv-8: build_edit_prompt does not include any raw OOXML XML in the LLM-facing prompt
invariants.inv-9: Output zip preserves [Content_Types].xml override entry order and binary parts byte-for-byte for unmodified content
invariants.inv-10: EditMetadata.source_prompt_hash is computed by M-EDIT, never trusted from the model output

required-signals.conditional-marker: [OOXML][autorepair][BLOCK_OOXML_AUTOREPAIR] — fires inside BLOCK_OOXML_PACK only when the input requires repair (durableId overflow, missing xml:space=preserve, malformed rsid). Optional in trace.

[Edit][tracked_change][BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE] — fires inside BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP only for ops with type in {tracked_replace, tracked_delete, accept_change, reject_change}. Optional in trace; required when at least one such op is in the plan.
required-signals.trace-sequence: BLOCK_EDIT_PLAN_VALIDATE -> BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP -> BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK -> BLOCK_EDIT_EXTRACT_TEXT -> BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR(per op) -> BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP(per op, may emit BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE) -> BLOCK_OOXML_PACK(may emit BLOCK_OOXML_AUTOREPAIR) -> BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS -> RETURNED

forbidden-signals.forbidden-1: Any opening OOXML element tag (regex /<[a-z][a-z0-9]*:[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]*(\s|>|\/)/ matching any of the OOXML namespaces w:, w14:, w15:, w16:, wp:, wps:, a:, m:, mc:, r:, pic:) inside the LLM prompt produced by build_edit_prompt — checked by unit test on rendered prompt string
forbidden-signals.forbidden-2: BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP fires before BLOCK_EDIT_PLAN_VALIDATE has emitted a success record
forbidden-signals.forbidden-3: BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK fires before BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP has emitted a success record
forbidden-signals.forbidden-4: Output written without a preceding BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS

VF-011.NAME: EngineDispatch
VF-011.scenario: Phase-6 dual-engine flag: any `mint <subcmd>` invocation routes through M-CLI._select_engine. With engine=js (env or --engine), legacy DF-001..DF-007 proceed unchanged. With engine=python (default in Phase 0), _select_engine raises NotImplementedError and no cmd_* handler runs.
VF-011.expected-outcome: (a) `--engine js` invocations remain trace-compatible with VF-001..VF-010; (b) default and explicit `--engine python` invocations exit non-zero with NotImplementedError naming Phase 0 + remediation, before any cmd_* / BLOCK_* legacy marker fires; (c) `--engine` always wins over MINT_ENGINE because M-CLI.main writes os.environ before config().

invariants.inv-1: MintConfig stays frozen; the CLI override channel is os.environ, not dataclass mutation.
invariants.inv-2: os.environ["MINT_ENGINE"] write (when --engine is set) precedes the FIRST config() call in main(); if config() is loaded before, the override is silently lost — forbidden.
invariants.inv-3: _select_engine returns Engine.JS or raises; never returns Engine.PYTHON or any other string.
invariants.inv-4: An invalid MINT_ENGINE value raises CONFIG_INVALID at load_config time, naming MINT_ENGINE in the message — never silently falls back to the default.

required-signals.log-marker: [Config][load][BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG] — added in Wave-6-1A (commit 2baab4a); INFO payload format `[Config][load][BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG] tier=%s severity=%s engine=%s sandbox_timeout=%d` via mint.config logger; wrapped by `# START_BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG`/`# END_BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG` source markers

[CLI][_select_engine][BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE] — added in Wave-6-1B (commit 73ae169) at src/mint/cli.py lines 422-426; fires once per main() between BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG and (on js path) cmd_* dispatch; INFO payload carries cfg.engine.value via mint.cli logger; absence on a non-help, non-error invocation is a bug

[CLI][main][BLOCK_DISPATCH] — added in cleanup commit at src/mint/cli.py main(); fires after _select_engine returns 'js' and before cmd_* dispatch; absent on python path because NotImplementedError raises first; INFO payload carries args.command
required-signals.trace-sequence: BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG -> BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE -> (engine==js) BLOCK_DISPATCH -> cmd_*; (engine==python) BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG -> BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE -> NotImplementedError -> non-zero exit

forbidden-signals.forbidden-1: Any cmd_* / BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS / BLOCK_ORCHESTRATE / BLOCK_EXECUTE_CODE / BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK / BLOCK_FILL marker firing on the python path
forbidden-signals.forbidden-2: NotImplementedError firing on the js path
forbidden-signals.forbidden-3: BLOCK_DISPATCH firing without a preceding BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE in the same process — proves the chokepoint was bypassed
forbidden-signals.forbidden-4: BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE firing twice in a single main() invocation — proves config() reloaded mid-flight

VF-011.chokepoint-scope: The `_select_engine` chokepoint gates ONLY two entry surfaces: (a) the `mint <subcmd>` CLI invocation (every cmd_* dispatch), and (b) the `mint serve` MCP server bootstrap (chokepoint fires once when the server process starts). It does NOT gate direct module imports. Tests under tests/integration/ that bypass the CLI by importing `mint.mcp_g1.mint_validate`, `mint.mcp_g2.mint_create`, `mint.edit.edit`, or `mint.create.create` directly are intentionally outside this contract — they exercise the underlying Python API which has no engine concept. A future fix-agent encountering such a direct import MUST NOT "patch" the test to route through the CLI. The chokepoint is a policy gate for end-user-facing entry points, not a security boundary on the Python API surface.
VF-011.serve-mode-invariant: For `mint serve` (long-running MCP server): _select_engine fires exactly once at server bootstrap, BEFORE the first MCP tool call is dispatched. Subsequent changes to MINT_ENGINE in the parent shell are silently ignored — the engine choice is frozen for the lifetime of the server process. This is intentional and matches the immutable-config invariant. To switch engines, restart the server.
VF-011.swarm-safety: SWARM-SAFE under monkeypatch.setenv (process-scoped) and pytest-xdist (process-per-worker). In-process serial test ordering MUST use the clean_env autouse fixture extended to include MINT_ENGINE; otherwise an os.environ write in main() leaks to sibling tests.

VF-012.NAME: PurePythonRegression
VF-012.ACTIVATES_AT: Phase-6 step beyond skeleton (handover Phase 1+ when MP-EXEC and MP-SDK ship code)
VF-012.scenario: Same prompt + same design tokens executed under MINT_ENGINE=js and MINT_ENGINE=python; outputs compared on validation pass-rate, structural fingerprint, and visual QA. Establishes the dual-runtime parity contract that gates flipping the JS engine to deprecated.
VF-012.expected-outcome: Python output matches or exceeds JS output on the showcase corpus per acceptance #1 of `docs/mint-pure-python-handover-v1.md` §7 ("≥ текущей JS-версии on 20+ test cases including showcase.docx"); GRACE Custom XML Parts read identically from both outputs.

invariants.inv-1: Python engine passes every existing fixture in tests/fixtures/ that the JS engine passes (no regressions on D-H, P-H, or GRACE rules)
invariants.inv-2: For every prompt in the parity corpus, M-VALIDATE reports identical violation sets (or a Python-superset) on both engines
invariants.inv-3: M-FINGERPRINT structural fingerprint of the Python output is within drift threshold of the JS output (theme1.xml + styles.xml hash families bounded — exact byte equality is NOT required because docx-js and python-docx serialize attributes in different orders)
invariants.inv-4: M-GRACE manifest + instructions parse to the same logical content from both outputs (Custom XML Parts under urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest)
invariants.inv-5: Visual QA L1+L2 (M-QA via Gotenberg) reports overlap and font-substitution rates within the same severity band on both engines

required-signals.log-marker: [CLI][_select_engine][BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE] — fires once per invocation, payload identifies engine
[Validate][run_checks][BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS] — fires on both engines' outputs, used to compare violation sets
required-signals.trace-sequence: For each parity-corpus prompt: run twice (engine=js, engine=python); both runs emit BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE → BLOCK_DISPATCH → cmd_create → BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS → RETURNED. Trace runs are independent processes; comparison is post-hoc on captured outputs.

forbidden-signals.forbidden-1: Python engine emits a violation that the JS engine does not, AND the violation is not classified as a known Python-engine improvement (whitelist maintained alongside the parity corpus)

VF-012.activation-gate: This VF cannot be exercised in Phase 0 because mint_python is empty. It MUST be wired up before mint_python.execution and mint_python.sdk land production code (handover Phase 1 boundary). Until activated, this VF is informational documentation of the dual-runtime parity contract.

baseline-lineage.phase-7-anchor: VF-013 PurePythonDocFlow + tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json — the EARLIEST python-engine baseline; pins a single-doc structural fingerprint and audit-mode soft-violation aggregates. NOT a parity baseline (no js-engine comparison). Activates at Wave-7-5.
baseline-lineage.phase-12-plus-anchor: VF-012 PurePythonRegression + tests/baseline/python-vs-js-showcase.json (PLANNED PATH; file does NOT exist yet) — the FULL parity baseline corpus mandated by handover §7 acceptance #1: ≥20 prompts including showcase.docx, run twice (engine=js, engine=python), structural fingerprint + M-VALIDATE violation set + M-FINGERPRINT drift envelope per pair. Activates when MP-EXEC-FRONTIER STATUS="done" AND Phase-4-execution-tiers shipped.
baseline-lineage.progression: VF-013 → VF-012: as MP-EXEC-* (handover §6 Phase 4) lands, the parity corpus expands from "one golden doc on python only" to "≥20 prompts on both engines". The Phase-7 baseline (mp_e2e_baseline.json) remains as a regression anchor; the Phase-4-plus baseline (python-vs-js-showcase.json) is the deprecation gate for M-SANDBOX (per VF-012 deprecation-trigger).

VF-012.deprecation-trigger: VF-012 green on the showcase corpus + handover rollout Phase 4 (Execution tiers) shipped = the conjunctive gate for M-SANDBOX `STATUS="deprecated"`. Both conditions must hold. Until then M-SANDBOX stays `STATUS="done"`. No calendar version is pinned here — version numbers slip naturally with the gate. Removal happens one minor version after deprecation lands. Phase-4-shipped boolean: grep `<MP-EXEC-FRONTIER ` and the small/medium MP-EXEC-* sibling tags in `docs/knowledge-graph.xml`; ALL must carry `STATUS="done"` (executable check via `grep -E 'MP-EXEC-(SMALL|MEDIUM|FRONTIER)[^>]*STATUS="done"' docs/knowledge-graph.xml | wc -l` returning 3).
VF-012.swarm-safety: Comparison runs are independent processes by construction; no shared mutable state across engines. SWARM-SAFE for parallel execution.

VF-013.NAME: PurePythonDocFlow
VF-013.scenario: Phase-7 acceptance: a 3-section golden document built via mint_python.sdk (Document.with_style_preset → add_cover → add_section × 2 with paragraph + table content → add_toc → save) produces a .docx that lxml parses cleanly AND passes M-VALIDATE on lenient mode (zero hard violations; aggregated soft violations stable against tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json).

golden-document-spec.title: VF-013 Golden Document

body-spec.cover: title="Q2 Memo", subtitle="Phase-7 acceptance fixture", logo=None

section-1.title: Summary
section-1.content-1: Paragraph: "This is the Phase-7 e2e fixture covering paragraph + table rendering through MP-CONTENT and MP-TABLE."

golden-document-spec.reproducibility: The function `tests/integration/test_mp_e2e.py::build_golden_document(tmp_path)` MUST be the single source of truth for this spec. Worker for Wave-7-5 implements this function; future agents MUST NOT inline the doc construction in test bodies — always call the helper.

VF-013.expected-outcome: (a) save() emits exactly one BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX with section_count=3 (cover + Summary + Details), table_count=2, paragraph_count=9 (cover title + cover subtitle + 2 section titles + 1 toc placeholder + 4 body paragraphs depending on cover/toc rendering — exact value pinned at first baseline run), output_path matching the requested path; (b) lxml.etree.parse(zipfile.ZipFile(...).open("word/document.xml")) succeeds without exception; (c) `mint.validate.validate(saved_path, MintConfig(severity_mode=SeverityMode.LENIENT, ...))` returns ValidationReport with passed=True and hard_count=0; (d) audit-mode soft violations grouped by rule_id+count match tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json::audit_violations; (e) Section.add_chart called anywhere raises NotImplementedError with BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD emitted first.

invariants.inv-1: IDEMPOTENCY: build_golden_document(tmp_path/"a") and build_golden_document(tmp_path/"b") produce two .docx files with equal mint.fingerprint.fingerprint().hash. Byte-equality NOT asserted (python-docx attribute order varies); the structural hash IS asserted.
invariants.inv-2: STRUCTURAL VALIDITY (Phase-7 level): word/document.xml MUST parse via lxml without raising; word/styles.xml MUST parse; the ZIP MUST contain at minimum {word/document.xml, word/styles.xml, [Content_Types].xml, _rels/.rels}. Full ECMA-376 schema validation is OUT OF SCOPE for Phase-7 (deferred to Phase-3 alongside MP-VALIDATE). A future agent MUST NOT downgrade this invariant by adding a "skipped because schema bundle absent" branch — keep it as lxml-parses-cleanly.
invariants.inv-3: NO GRACE CUSTOM XML PARTS: ZIP entries matching `customXml/item*.xml` either do NOT exist OR exist but contain no payload under the `urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest` namespace. Phase-5 (MP-GRACE) will introduce these; Phase-7 must NOT preempt.
invariants.inv-4: PRESET IS OBSERVABLE: lxml-parse word/styles.xml; the Heading 1 style entry's run-properties carry rFonts.ascii="Inter" and color.val="0F4C81" (or whatever alga_corporate.typography.heading1 resolves to). Pinned in the baseline JSON's `audit_violations` ONLY indirectly — assert directly via lxml XPath, not via fingerprint.
invariants.inv-5: BASELINE HASH PIN: structural_fingerprint_sha256 matches tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json::structural_fingerprint_sha256. First run writes the baseline (gated by MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1). Subsequent runs assert equality.
invariants.inv-6: NO LEGACY SANDBOX TOUCHED: under the `--engine js` regression suite (existing tests/integration/test_cli.py with worker D's _run pin), VF-013 does NOT execute and the legacy js path remains green. Confirms VF-013 is python-engine-only.

required-signals.log-marker: [MP-Style][load_preset][BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET] — fires exactly once per Document.with_style_preset call; payload: preset_name + source (registry|file)
[MP-Document][save][BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX] — fires exactly once per save() call; payload: section_count, table_count, output_path
[MP-Content][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CONTENT] — fires once per Paragraph or Image .render(); count must equal the paragraph_count + image_count emitted from BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX
[MP-Table][render][BLOCK_RENDER_TABLE] — fires exactly twice for the golden doc (one per add_table)
required-signals.conditional-marker: [MP-Section][add_chart][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] / [MP-Document][stub][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] — fires whenever a stub method is called; payload: method_name + target_phase. NEVER fires on the golden-doc path (no stub touched).
required-signals.trace-sequence: Per build_golden_document run, the ordered sequence is: BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET → (BLOCK_RENDER_TABLE | BLOCK_RENDER_CONTENT)+ → BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX. Counts: BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET=1, BLOCK_RENDER_TABLE=2, BLOCK_RENDER_CONTENT≥4 (one per body paragraph; cover/toc paragraphs may be rendered through python-docx primitives without emitting MP-Content marker — pinned in baseline), BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX=1, total BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD=0.

forbidden-signals.forbidden-1: BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX firing more than once for a single Document.save call
forbidden-signals.forbidden-2: NotImplementedError raised on a non-stub method (would indicate a regression in concrete implementations)
forbidden-signals.forbidden-3: BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD firing without an immediately following NotImplementedError (stub MUST raise)
forbidden-signals.forbidden-4: BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD firing on the golden-doc happy path (golden doc uses no stubs; presence here is a regression where a concrete method delegated to a stub)
forbidden-signals.forbidden-5: Any [Sandbox][...], [Validate][run_checks][...] (other than the explicit M-VALIDATE invocation), or [Edit][...] log markers — the golden-doc construction path MUST NOT touch the legacy engine surface

e2e-harness-spec.fixture: tmp_path (pytest builtin) — used for both the saved .docx and any baseline writes
e2e-harness-spec.helper: build_golden_document(out_path: Path) -> Path — single source of truth for the golden doc spec; returns the path of the saved file
e2e-harness-spec.validation-mode: Lenient via `mint.validate.validate(saved_path, MintConfig(severity_mode=SeverityMode.LENIENT, llm_base_url="http://x:1", llm_model="m", model_tier=Tier.SMALL))` — direct in-process call (NOT subprocess; faster and avoids env propagation flake)
e2e-harness-spec.baseline-write-gate: If `os.environ.get("MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE") == "1"`, write the JSON; otherwise read-only assert. Pytest exit code on missing baseline file = FAIL with explicit "set MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1 on the first run" hint.
e2e-harness-spec.log-capture: caplog with caplog.set_level(logging.INFO); records collected and filtered by marker substring; counts asserted via Counter([m.split(']')[2].split('[')[1] for m in messages]) or similar — implementation detail of the test, the contract is the marker counts above

VF-013.activation-gate: This VF is exercised at Wave-7-5 (the e2e closing wave). Earlier waves run only their module-local checks. Gate-Phase-7 references VF-013 as the headline acceptance signal.
VF-013.swarm-safety: SWARM-SAFE: e2e test writes to tmp_path; no shared mutable state; M-VALIDATE and M-FINGERPRINT invocations are pure read-only on the saved file. Baseline write gated by MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE env so concurrent xdist workers cannot race-overwrite the baseline JSON. Module-local waves (7-1..7-4) are read-only against the rest of the repo and SAFE for pytest-xdist.

scenarios.scenario-1: loadConfig reads LLM_BASE_URL + LLM_MODEL + MINT_MODEL_TIER from env → valid MintConfig
scenarios.scenario-2: loadConfig reads .env file when env vars absent → valid MintConfig
scenarios.scenario-3: Missing LLM_BASE_URL raises CONFIG_MISSING with message containing "LLM_BASE_URL"
scenarios.scenario-4: Invalid MINT_MODEL_TIER (e.g. "huge") raises CONFIG_INVALID with valid options listed
scenarios.scenario-5: (Phase-6) MINT_ENGINE unset → MintConfig.engine == Engine.PYTHON (default)
scenarios.scenario-7: (Phase-6) MINT_ENGINE="garbage" raises CONFIG_INVALID with message containing "MINT_ENGINE" and listing {python, js}

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Never returns None — must raise or return valid MintConfig
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Never logs API keys at INFO level
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: (Phase-6) MintConfig must remain frozen — engine field must not be mutable post-construction
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: (Phase-6) CENTRAL FIXTURE INVARIANT: any test file that reads or sets MINT_ENGINE (via os.environ, monkeypatch.setenv, dotenv, or subprocess env) MUST import the central `clean_env` autouse fixture from tests/unit/test_config.py via conftest.py / fixture import — NOT redefine its own `clean_env` locally. Local redefinition without `MINT_ENGINE` in env_keys creates silent leak between sibling tests AND silently passes V-MP-FLAG scenario-11 (which only inspects the central fixture). Enforcement command (suitable for pre-commit hook or CI step): `[ "$(grep -rl '^def clean_env' tests/unit/ 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')" = "1" ] && grep -l '^def clean_env' tests/unit/ | grep -q '^tests/unit/test_config\.py$'` — exits 0 only when exactly one file matches AND that file is `tests/unit/test_config.py`. This invariant is the single chokepoint that prevents the swarm-flake vector identified by peer review.

V-M-CONFIG.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — no external deps, pure config loading, fully deterministic. (Phase-6 Wave-6-1 onward) SWARM-SAFE only when forbidden-4 is enforced — central clean_env fixture is the single point keeping MINT_ENGINE state isolated between in-process pytest tests.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: process.exit() in user code must be rejected
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Never write files outside designated output directory

scenarios.scenario-7: (Phase-5) mint edit fixture.docx --plan plan.json prints EditResult JSON; output_path inside the JSON points to an existing file

scenarios.scenario-9: (Phase-6) mint --engine python validate fixture.docx — same as scenario-8
scenarios.scenario-10: (Phase-6) MINT_ENGINE=js mint validate fixture.docx — succeeds (env supplies engine)
scenarios.scenario-11: (Phase-6) MINT_ENGINE=python mint --engine js validate fixture.docx — succeeds (CLI overrides env via os.environ pre-load)
scenarios.scenario-12: (Phase-6) mint --help shows top-level `--engine {python,js}` flag with help text mentioning MINT_ENGINE
scenarios.scenario-13: (Phase-6) NEGATIVE TRACE: mint validate fixture.docx (no --engine, no MINT_ENGINE) — captured stderr contains [CLI][_select_engine][BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE] and NotImplementedError but does NOT contain any [Validate], [Create], [Sandbox], [OOXML], [Edit], or BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS / BLOCK_ORCHESTRATE / BLOCK_EXECUTE_CODE / BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK markers. Proves the chokepoint runs BEFORE any cmd_* would have started side effects.
scenarios.scenario-14: (Phase-6) ARG POSITION: mint validate --engine js fixture.docx (--engine after subcommand) → CLI_USAGE_ERROR (non-zero), argparse usage to stderr. --engine is top-level only.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never crash with an unhandled exception leaking traceback to stdout (stderr is acceptable for genuine bugs)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never print non-JSON content to stdout for the JSON-emitting subcommands

V-M-CLI.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — thin dispatcher; integration tests cover end-to-end behavior

scenarios.scenario-1: unpack(minimal_valid_docx) → returns parts list including [Content_Types].xml, word/document.xml, word/styles.xml; all XML files parse without errors
scenarios.scenario-2: unpack → pack → unpack round-trip on minimal_valid_docx: part listing identical, every XML part tree-equal, every binary part byte-equal
scenarios.scenario-3: merge_runs collapses two adjacent w:r with identical w:rPr into one; never merges across w:hyperlink, w:bookmarkStart, w:bookmarkEnd, w:commentRangeStart, w:commentRangeEnd, w:commentReference, w:ins, w:del, w:fldChar, w:tab, w:br, w:sym, or any element introducing a non-text child between the runs
scenarios.scenario-4: escape_smart_quotes converts ASCII apostrophes to ’ in body text but skips runs with rStyle ending in 'Code'; idempotent on already-escaped input
scenarios.scenario-4b: escape_smart_quotes alternates double quotes per paragraph: first ASCII '"' → “, second → ”, third → “, etc. Unpaired (odd) double quote in a paragraph is left as ASCII '"'. Reset across paragraphs. Mixed apostrophes and double quotes counted independently.
scenarios.scenario-5: pack auto-repair: input with w:durableId=0xFFFFFFFE produces output with regenerated valid id; preserved_whitespace_runs increments for each w:t with leading/trailing whitespace lacking xml:space=preserve
scenarios.scenario-8: validate_relationships on unpack tree with dangling Target raises OOXML_RELATIONSHIP_BROKEN naming the missing target
scenarios.scenario-9: [Content_Types].xml Override entry order in pack output equals input order exactly

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never invoke LibreOffice, soffice, or any external converter
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never silently drop or rename a part during round-trip
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Must never double-escape an existing XML entity

V-M-OOXML.autonomy-assessment: SAFE — pure stdlib zipfile + lxml manipulation, deterministic, no LLM

scenarios.scenario-1: validate_plan(EditPlan with 1 replace_text op + valid anchor) passes
scenarios.scenario-2: validate_plan(EditPlan with empty ops) raises EDIT_PLAN_INVALID
scenarios.scenario-3: validate_plan(op with anchor.value containing ASCII control characters or exceeding 512 chars) raises EDIT_PLAN_INVALID
scenarios.scenario-4: edit(minimal_valid_docx, replace_text "Hello" → "Hi") produces output passing M-VALIDATE; before_snippet/after_snippet captured in diff
scenarios.scenario-5: resolve_anchor on text appearing twice without context_before/after raises EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS
scenarios.scenario-6: resolve_anchor on text not present raises EDIT_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND
scenarios.scenario-7: edit with insert_paragraph(style="Heading2") after first paragraph: output has new w:p with w:pStyle w:val="Heading2", validates clean
scenarios.scenario-8: tracked_replace op produces sibling w:del + w:ins with author/date attributes; w:rPr preserved on both branches
scenarios.scenario-9: tracked_delete on full paragraph also marks paragraph mark via w:pPr/w:rPr/w:del
scenarios.scenario-10: add_comment with parent_id=0 creates a top-level commentRangeStart/End pair around the target plus a sibling commentRangeStart/End pair for the reply; the reply linkage lives in word/commentsExtended.xml as w15:commentEx with paraIdParent matching the parent paraId; comments.xml carries both top-level and reply text; content types registered
scenarios.scenario-11: add_comment respects "commentRangeStart/End are siblings of w:r, never inside w:r" invariant — schema check on document.xml confirms
scenarios.scenario-12: accept_change on an insertion by id removes the w:ins wrapper and keeps inserted text; accept_change on a deletion by id removes the w:del subtree (deleted text is gone); reject_change on an insertion drops the w:ins subtree (inserted text gone); reject_change on a deletion removes the w:del wrapper and restores w:t from w:delText
scenarios.scenario-13: build_edit_prompt does not include any raw OOXML XML in the LLM-facing prompt — only extracted text + stable anchors + op schema
scenarios.scenario-14: execute_op on an op carrying an unsupported type (for example type="rotate_image" in v0) raises EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED before any tree mutation
scenarios.scenario-15: edit run end-to-end: backup created, M-VALIDATE invoked on output, EditResult.success=True, diff.length == ops_total
scenarios.scenario-16: set_paragraph_style with style_id="Heading2" on body paragraph: output paragraph has w:pStyle w:val="Heading2", original w:rPr preserved on existing runs, M-VALIDATE clean
scenarios.scenario-17: set_paragraph_style with style_id missing from styles.xml raises EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED before mutation
scenarios.scenario-18: delete_paragraph on anchored paragraph removes the w:p subtree; relationships referenced only from that paragraph are removed from document.xml.rels; M-VALIDATE clean
scenarios.scenario-19: paragraph_index anchors resolve against the original tree even after earlier ops in the same plan inserted or deleted paragraphs (no re-indexing)
scenarios.scenario-20: EditMetadata.source_prompt_hash is computed by M-EDIT (not read from model output) and is stable for identical inputs
scenarios.scenario-21: EditPlan with op-1 delete_paragraph(anchor=A) followed by op-2 anchored at A by paragraph_index/hash/text: op-2 raises EDIT_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND because the original-tree anchor for A no longer resolves after op-1 mutated the tree, surfacing the documented "no re-indexing across ops" semantics
scenarios.scenario-22: edit(severity_mode="strict") on a fixture that produces a soft violation in the output triggers EDIT_VALIDATION_FAILED and refuses to write output_path; backup remains intact
scenarios.scenario-23: EditPlan JSON uses 'type' as the discriminator field on both EditOp and Anchor (not 'op_type' and not 'kind') — schema test asserts no other discriminator name appears in the schema
scenarios.scenario-24: resolve_anchor with type='hash' on a tree where two different paragraphs share the same 8-hex SHA1 prefix raises EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS; the runtime never silently picks the first match. Test mechanics: a real 8-hex SHA1 collision is impractical to brute-force, so the test monkeypatches mint.edit._paragraph_hash (or equivalent helper) to return a fixed value 'deadbeef' for two distinct paragraphs in a fresh fixture; the assertion is that resolve_anchor raises EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS with both paragraph_index values listed in the error message.
scenarios.scenario-25: edit when the backup destination is unwritable (read-only directory or permission denied) raises BACKUP_FAILED before any unpack or tree mutation; the original document is unchanged on disk
scenarios.scenario-26: edit with output_path omitted writes to '<input-stem>.edited<ext>' next to the input; backup is at '<input>.bak'; output_path != backup_path
scenarios.scenario-27: edit on an EditPlan with format='pptx' raises EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED before any tree mutation (PPTX edits are deferred to v0.5)
scenarios.scenario-28: successful edit emits BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP exactly once and strictly before BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK; the backup file exists on disk with identical bytes to the original input
scenarios.scenario-29: successful edit emits BLOCK_EDIT_EXTRACT_TEXT exactly once and strictly before the first BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR; the rendered prompt for an LLM-driven plan is stable for identical input + anchor list

required-log-markers.marker-7: [Edit][tracked_change][BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE] (conditional — only when at least one op type is in {tracked_replace, tracked_delete, accept_change, reject_change})
required-log-markers.trace-sequence: BLOCK_EDIT_PLAN_VALIDATE -> BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP -> BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK -> BLOCK_EDIT_EXTRACT_TEXT -> BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR(per op) -> BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP(per op, may emit BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE) -> BLOCK_OOXML_PACK -> BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS -> RETURNED

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Must never expose raw OOXML XML to the LLM in build_edit_prompt
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Must never apply operations if validate_plan failed
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: Must never write output if M-VALIDATE on the result fails in strict mode without surfacing EDIT_VALIDATION_FAILED
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: Must never modify tracked-change entries authored by other users beyond the documented per-id accept_change/reject_change ops
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: Must never proceed when anchor resolution is ambiguous; must raise EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS

V-M-EDIT.autonomy-assessment: CAUTIOUS — depends on external LLM for plan generation, but plan is typed JSON and every op is validated and bounded; backup is mandatory before any tree mutation

module-checks.check-1: python -c "import mint_python; import mint_python.core, mint_python.sdk, mint_python.execution, mint_python.rules, mint_python.grace, mint_python.mcp, mint_python.cli, mint_python.tests"

scenarios.scenario-1: All 9 entry points (top-level + 8 subpackages) import without error; exit 0
scenarios.scenario-2: mint_python.__version__ == "0.0.0"
scenarios.scenario-3: mint_python and every subpackage are listed in `unzip -l dist/*.whl` after `uv build`. Phase-level only — module-level workers do not run uv build per change.
scenarios.scenario-4: Each subpackage __init__.py contains the repo's `# FILE:` header convention (grep `^# FILE: src/mint_python/`). Forbids drift to one-line-docstring style.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: No subpackage may export a public function/class in Phase 0 — contracts populate in Phases 1-5; an early export risks contract drift before code lands
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: __init__.py files must not import from src/mint/* — the skeleton stays decoupled from the legacy package
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: PYTHON-FIRST POLICY (constraint-8 in requirements.xml): no MP-* module may invoke Node.js — no `subprocess` calls into `node`/`npm`/`vm2`/`isolated-vm`, no IPC bridges into `src/mint/sandbox/runner.js`, no shims that wrap docx-js. Allowed external processes from MP-* code: HTTP services (Gotenberg in Phase-5) and the future RestrictedPython sandbox (Phase-4). Enforcement command: `grep -rnE "(subprocess|os\.system|os\.popen|os\.spawn).*(node|npm|vm2|isolated-vm|src/mint/sandbox)" src/mint_python/ 2>/dev/null` returns zero matches; CI hook recommended.

V-MP-PKG.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — empty package skeleton; idempotent file creation, no shared mutable state, no env reads. A worker creating these files cannot interfere with another worker editing src/mint/ as long as both stay within their declared write scope (worker-owns / controller-owns split in development-plan.xml ExecutionPolicy).

V-MP-FLAG.note: Cross-cuts M-CONFIG (Engine enum + field + parsing) and M-CLI (--engine arg + os.environ write + _select_engine chokepoint). Hosted as a separate V-* entry so a single failure packet maps cleanly to the feature; V-M-CONFIG and V-M-CLI cross-reference it via their test-files lists.

swarm-fixtures.fixture-1: Reuse the `clean_env` autouse fixture from tests/unit/test_config.py (copy or import). MUST extend its env_keys list with "MINT_ENGINE" before any test in this file runs. Without this extension, an os.environ write inside one test (e.g. via subprocess or main()) leaks into a sibling pytest test run in the same process.
swarm-fixtures.fixture-2: For subprocess scenarios use `subprocess.run([..., env={...minimal env without MINT_ENGINE..., **overrides}], capture_output=True)`. NEVER inherit os.environ — that defeats isolation across pytest workers when one worker pre-set the var.

scenarios.scenario-1: MintConfig with required env set, MINT_ENGINE unset → engine == Engine.PYTHON (default)
scenarios.scenario-2: monkeypatch.setenv("MINT_ENGINE", "js"); load_config() → engine == Engine.JS
scenarios.scenario-3: monkeypatch.setenv("MINT_ENGINE", "garbage"); load_config() raises ConfigInvalidError; message contains the literal substring "MINT_ENGINE" and lists both valid values "python" and "js"
scenarios.scenario-4: _select_engine(MintConfig(engine=Engine.JS)) returns "js" without raising
scenarios.scenario-5: _select_engine(MintConfig(engine=Engine.PYTHON)) raises NotImplementedError; message contains both "MINT_ENGINE=python" and "Phase 0"
scenarios.scenario-6: subprocess: env={MINT_ENGINE=python, ...required}, args=[python, -m, mint.cli, --engine, js, --help] → exit 0; stdout contains "--engine"
scenarios.scenario-7: FROZEN INVARIANT: cfg = load_config(); pytest.raises(dataclasses.FrozenInstanceError): cfg.engine = Engine.JS — proves the dataclass is frozen and the override channel is os.environ, not setattr
scenarios.scenario-8: ARG POSITION: subprocess `python -m mint.cli validate --engine js fixture.docx` (--engine AFTER subcommand) → non-zero exit, argparse usage error to stderr. Confirms --engine is top-level only and does not silently accept post-subcommand placement.
scenarios.scenario-9: CLI override precedence: subprocess with env MINT_ENGINE=python, args [..., --engine, js, validate, ...] → captured stderr/log shows BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG entry where engine resolved to "js", proving os.environ write in main() ran BEFORE config() was first called. (Asserts on the log payload, not on the side effect alone.)
scenarios.scenario-10: Default-engine path: subprocess with no MINT_ENGINE, no --engine, args [..., validate, fixture] → stderr contains BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG and BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE markers; does NOT contain BLOCK_DISPATCH or any [Validate]/[Create]/[Sandbox] block markers; exit non-zero with NotImplementedError surfaced.
scenarios.scenario-11: Env-isolation invariant: extend tests/unit/test_config.py::clean_env env_keys to include "MINT_ENGINE"; assert that running this V-MP-FLAG suite immediately followed by tests/unit/test_config.py with no isolation passes — i.e., no test in this file leaks MINT_ENGINE into os.environ for the next test.
scenarios.scenario-12: .ENV-FILE PRECEDENCE: write a temp .env containing `MINT_ENGINE=python`; with os.environ["MINT_ENGINE"]="js" set, call load_config(env_file=tmp) → engine == Engine.JS. Confirms os.environ wins over .env file (mirrors the existing dotenv_values + os.environ overlay pattern in load_config). Inverse case (no env override, only .env): engine == Engine.PYTHON from the .env value.
scenarios.scenario-13: PYPROJECT.TOML SHAPE: `python -c "import tomllib; d=tomllib.load(open('pyproject.toml','rb')); assert d['tool']['hatch']['build']['targets']['wheel']['packages']==['src/mint','src/mint_python']"` exits 0. Module-level guard catches typos in the packages list before phase-level uv build would.
scenarios.scenario-14: .ENV.EXAMPLE DOCS: `.env.example` contains a `# MINT_ENGINE=js` commented opt-in line; grep `-E "^# *MINT_ENGINE=" .env.example` matches exactly once. Forbids the file shipping without engine documentation when the default fail-closed is python.

required-log-markers.marker-1: [Config][load][BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG] — payload extended (Phase-6) to include resolved engine value at INFO
required-log-markers.marker-2: [CLI][_select_engine][BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE] — NEW; emitted on every non-help/non-usage-error main() before dispatch; INFO level

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: NotImplementedError raised on the js path
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: config() loaded before os.environ override is applied (CLI override silently dropped)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: Any test in this file mutates os.environ without using monkeypatch.setenv (must be reverted automatically by pytest)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: Subprocess scenarios that inherit the parent os.environ for MINT_ENGINE — defeats parallel worker isolation

V-MP-FLAG.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE provided the clean_env fixture is extended (fixture-1) and subprocess scenarios pass explicit env (fixture-2). Without these, parallel test workers in the same process can leak MINT_ENGINE between tests via main()'s os.environ write, producing flaky failures. Process-level workers (pytest-xdist -n auto) are inherently safe because each worker is a fresh process. Document the in-process risk explicitly in the test file header so a future contributor (or fix-agent) does not strip the fixture.

V-MP-STYLE.schema-reference: docs/style-preset-schema.md (normative); presets must validate against the JSON Schema described there. The schema is the single source of truth for required keys, color hex format, palette tokens (@primary etc.), StyleSpec defaults, and SemVer versioning rules.

scenarios.scenario-1: Pt(12) returns 240 (twentieths of a point); Pt(0.5) returns 10
scenarios.scenario-2: ColorPalette('alga_corporate').resolve('primary') returns the documented hex (e.g. '#0F4C81'); unknown name raises KeyError naming the palette + missing color
scenarios.scenario-3: load_preset('alga_corporate') returns a Style namespace with heading1, heading2, body, table_header, caption all populated as frozen Style instances
scenarios.scenario-4: load_preset(path=tmp_json) loads from arbitrary file when JSON validates against schema
scenarios.scenario-5: load_preset('does_not_exist') raises STYLE_PRESET_NOT_FOUND naming the registry contents
scenarios.scenario-6: load_preset(path=invalid_schema_json) raises STYLE_PRESET_INVALID_SCHEMA; message contains a JSON Pointer (e.g. "/typography/heading1/color") AND the constraint name. Test parametrizes 4+ violations: missing required key (color_palette.primary), bad hex format ("rgb(...)"), wrong type (size_pt as string), wrong enum (alignment="diagonal").
scenarios.scenario-7: Style frozen invariant: pytest.raises(FrozenInstanceError): style.color_hex = '#fff' — proves Style cannot be mutated post-construction
scenarios.scenario-8: load_preset emits [MP-Style][load_preset][BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET] with name + source payload
scenarios.scenario-9: PALETTE TOKEN RESOLUTION: a StyleSpec carrying color="@primary" resolves through the preset's own color_palette during load_preset; the returned Style instance has color_hex="#0F4C81" (or whatever primary is). Round-trip: re-serializing must NOT replace the literal hex with the @token (loaders don't compress).
scenarios.scenario-10: DANGLING TOKEN: a preset whose StyleSpec uses color="@nonexistent" raises STYLE_PRESET_INVALID_SCHEMA pointing at the typography field that referenced the missing palette key; message names the missing token.
scenarios.scenario-11: DEFAULTS: a StyleSpec with only the three required fields (font, size_pt, color) loads without error and Style instance has bold=False, italic=False, alignment="left", spacing_before_pt=0, spacing_after_pt=0, line_height=1.15, keep_with_next=False — exactly the schema-documented defaults.
scenarios.scenario-12: VERSION MISMATCH: a preset with `"version": "2.0"` raises STYLE_PRESET_INVALID_SCHEMA naming "version" and the supported major (Phase-7 supports 1.x).
scenarios.scenario-13: REGISTRY INTEGRITY: at module import, all three built-in presets (alga_corporate, minimal, compact) validate against the schema. A pytest test enumerates `mint_python.sdk.presets` and calls load_preset on each; all return non-empty Style namespaces with the six required typography keys.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Style instance must remain frozen — mutating any field after construction is forbidden
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: load_preset must never return a partially-populated Style namespace — all required keys (heading1..caption) must be present or the call must raise
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: SWARM-SAFETY: MP-STYLE must NOT introduce a module-level mutable singleton. The preset registry IS module-level, but it is read-only; load_preset(path=...) returns a fresh Style namespace WITHOUT updating the registry. Enforcement: `python -c "import mint_python.sdk; r = mint_python.sdk.presets; r2 = mint_python.sdk.presets; assert r is r2 and frozenset(r) == frozenset(['alga_corporate','minimal','compact'])"` exits 0 in any test order.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: load_preset MUST NOT silently fall through to a partial Style on schema violation — raising is mandatory; this prevents downstream MP-CONTENT/MP-TABLE renders against undefined Style fields.

V-MP-STYLE.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure deterministic; no I/O beyond JSON file reads in tmp; no env reads; module-level state is read-only registry

scenarios.scenario-1: Paragraph('hello').render(doc) appends a w:p element with a single w:r containing 'hello'
scenarios.scenario-2: Paragraph fluent: Paragraph('a').add_run('b', style=bold_style) returns self; renders two w:r with distinct rPr
scenarios.scenario-3: Paragraph inherits Style.body when no per-run style provided
scenarios.scenario-4: Image.from_path(tmp_png).render(doc) produces a w:drawing with a unique rId; pillow loads the image to compute aspect ratio
scenarios.scenario-5: Image.from_path('/nonexistent.png') raises IMAGE_FILE_NOT_FOUND
scenarios.scenario-6: Image.from_bytes(b'\x00bad', format='png') raises IMAGE_FORMAT_UNSUPPORTED OR a pillow-derived error wrapped with the path/format context
scenarios.scenario-7: Image width=Inches(3) sets the OOXML extent in EMU correctly (3 * 914400)

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Content][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CONTENT] — fires per Paragraph or Image .render() call

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Paragraph or Image must NEVER write to the filesystem during render — only into the python-docx Document object
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Run must not be re-used across Paragraphs (would corrupt OOXML). Render() may consume the Run; subsequent renders raise.

V-MP-CONTENT.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — render() target is a per-test python-docx Document instance; no shared mutable state

scenarios.scenario-1: Table.from_list([['a','b'],['1','2']]).render(doc) creates 2 rows × 2 cols; first row treated as header when header=True (default)
scenarios.scenario-2: Table.from_markdown('| a | b |\n|---|---|\n| 1 | 2 |') produces equivalent table
scenarios.scenario-3: Table.from_list_of_dicts([{'a':1,'b':2},{'a':3,'b':4}]) emits 3 rows × 2 cols (header + 2 data rows); column order follows insertion order of first dict
scenarios.scenario-4: Table.financial(rows) right-aligns numeric cells AND applies thousands separator (1000 → '1,000')
scenarios.scenario-5: Table.comparison(left='Q1', right='Q2', rows=[...]) produces a 2-column table with left/right headers
scenarios.scenario-6: apply_style(preset_table) overrides cell style; set_column_widths([2,3]) sets column widths in inches; autofit=True sets the python-docx table autofit flag
scenarios.scenario-7: Table.from_list([['a','b'],['1']]) raises TABLE_RAGGED_ROWS naming the bad row index
scenarios.scenario-8: Table.from_markdown('not a markdown table') raises TABLE_MARKDOWN_PARSE_ERROR
scenarios.scenario-9: Table.from_list_of_dicts([{'a':1},{'b':2}]) raises TABLE_INVALID_DICT_KEYS naming the diverging keys (rows must share the same key set)

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Table][render][BLOCK_RENDER_TABLE] — fires once per Table.render() call; payload: row_count, col_count

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: Factories must validate row shape before constructing Table — never return a partially-built Table that would raise mid-render
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: financial() must not silently coerce non-numeric cells; non-numerics keep their raw value and skip thousands separator

V-MP-TABLE.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure construction + render onto per-test Document; no shared state

scenarios.scenario-1: Section('Title', level=1).add_paragraph('text') returns self (fluent chain works)
scenarios.scenario-2: Section.render(doc) emits a w:p with heading style matching level (Heading 1, Heading 2, ...) followed by the content blocks in order
scenarios.scenario-3: add_paragraph accepts both str (shorthand) and Paragraph instance
scenarios.scenario-4: Section('X', level=10) raises SECTION_LEVEL_OUT_OF_RANGE (valid: 1..6)
scenarios.scenario-5: Section('X').add_chart(any) raises NotImplementedError; message contains "Phase 2" and "matplotlib"
scenarios.scenario-6: Section.add_chart emits [MP-Section][add_chart][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] with method='add_chart' and target_phase='Phase 2' BEFORE raising

required-log-markers.marker-1: [MP-Section][add_chart][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] — fires on stub touch; payload: method + target_phase

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: add_chart must raise immediately after emitting BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD — never silently no-op
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: Fluent methods must NEVER mutate other Section instances — each .add_* mutates self only

V-MP-SECTION.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure construction and per-test render

scenarios.scenario-1: Document(format='docx', title='X').with_style_preset('alga_corporate') returns self
scenarios.scenario-2: add_cover/add_section/add_toc/set_header/set_footer all return self for chaining; stored in declared order
scenarios.scenario-3: save(tmp_path / 'out.docx') produces a file that exists; lxml parses word/document.xml without error
scenarios.scenario-4: Document(format='pdf') raises DOCUMENT_FORMAT_UNSUPPORTED naming valid formats {'docx'} for Phase-1
scenarios.scenario-5: Document(...).with_style_preset('does_not_exist') raises DOCUMENT_PRESET_NOT_FOUND
scenarios.scenario-6: Each stub (inject_grace, validate, fix, to_pdf) raises NotImplementedError with target phase in message; emits BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD before raising
scenarios.scenario-7: save() emits exactly one [MP-Document][save][BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX] with section_count, table_count, output_path payload
scenarios.scenario-8: IDEMPOTENCY UNDER M-FINGERPRINT: invoking save() twice on the same Document produces two files whose `mint.fingerprint.fingerprint(path).hash` are equal. Pytest snippet (lives in tests/unit/test_mp_document.py): ``` from mint.fingerprint import fingerprint doc = build_doc(...) doc.save(tmp_path/"a.docx"); doc.save(tmp_path/"b.docx") assert fingerprint(tmp_path/"a.docx").hash == fingerprint(tmp_path/"b.docx").hash ``` Byte-equality is NOT asserted — python-docx writes attributes in non-deterministic order; the M-FINGERPRINT structural hash is the canonical equivalence relation. Failure mode: if hashes differ across two save() calls of the same Document, save() carries hidden non-determinism (e.g. uuid in cover, timestamp in header) — the test points at the diverging part by re-running with fingerprint().xml_sources to isolate which part of the ZIP changed.
scenarios.scenario-9: AUDIT-BASELINE PIN (Wave-7-5 e2e shape; mirrored here for module-local sanity): the golden 3-section document built per VF-013 produces a structural fingerprint that matches `tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json::structural_fingerprint_sha256`. First-ever run writes the baseline (with explicit `MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1` env, gated to prevent accidental overwrites). Subsequent runs assert equality; on mismatch the failure prints the new vs. old hash AND the diff of fingerprint().xml_sources to localize the regression.

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: save() must never produce a partial file: on serialization error, the temp output is removed before re-raising
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX firing more than once for a single save() call is forbidden (would indicate retry without state cleanup)
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-3: SWARM-SAFETY: MP-DOCUMENT must NOT introduce a module-level mutable singleton. Document instances are explicit per-call; no `_default_document` or registry. Tests MUST use tmp_path for every save target — never write to a stable repo path. Enforcement: `grep -rn 'output\.docx\|/tmp/[a-z]*\.docx' tests/unit/test_mp_document.py tests/integration/test_mp_e2e.py` returns zero matches outside tmp_path fixtures.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-4: save() output must NEVER include GRACE Custom XML Parts in Phase-7 (those are Phase-5/MP-GRACE territory). VF-013 inv-3 enforces this; a regression here would silently break the Phase-5 contract.
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-5: NON-DETERMINISM: save() must NOT introduce timestamps, UUIDs, or random IDs into the output beyond what python-docx itself emits at fixed values. If python-docx ever starts emitting per-call timestamps (e.g. for core.xml created/modified fields), MP-DOCUMENT MUST override these to a fixed sentinel value to preserve idempotency under M-FINGERPRINT.

field.name: schema_version
field.description: Baseline format version; bumped on shape changes

field.name: committed_at
field.description: ISO date of baseline commit; informational

field.name: generator
field.description: Tool versions used to generate baseline

field.name: document_spec
field.description: Reference to the function that builds the golden doc; future agents must rebuild via this same function

field.name: xml_sources
field.description: Parts contributing to the fingerprint hash; recorded so a future drift can be localized

field.name: counts
field.description: Structural counts from BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX payload + lxml introspection

field.name: audit_violations
field.description: M-VALIDATE audit-mode violations grouped by rule_id+severity (NOT individual instances — instances drift across python-docx versions; aggregated counts are stable)

field.name: hard_violation_count
field.description: MUST be 0 for VF-013 lenient acceptance; tracked separately for fast-fail

field.name: schema_validation
field.description: lxml_parses asserts word/document.xml parses without raising; ecma376_validated=false for Phase-7 (full ECMA-376 schema validation deferred to Phase-3)

baseline-update-protocol.step-1: Set `MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1` in the test invocation env to allow overwrite (default is read-only assertion).
baseline-update-protocol.step-2: Manually inspect the diff: structural_fingerprint_sha256 change WITHOUT a corresponding intentional MP-* change is a regression flag, not a baseline update.
baseline-update-protocol.step-3: Commit the new baseline in the same PR that ships the corresponding MP-* code change; commit message MUST reference the rationale (e.g. "MP-DOCUMENT: switch heading rendering from Title to Heading 1; baseline rotated").
baseline-update-protocol.step-4: Audit-violation rotation: a baseline that grows hard_violation_count above 0 is a Phase-7 acceptance failure — no rotation; fix the regression.

V-MP-DOCUMENT.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — save() targets tmp_path per test; no shared mutable state. e2e wave (Wave-7-5) reads-only against M-VALIDATE; baseline assertion is deterministic. Worker-write to tests/fixtures/mp_e2e_baseline.json is gated by MP_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE env so concurrent workers cannot race-overwrite.

scenarios.scenario-1: All §3 names import: `from mint_python.sdk import Document, Section, Table, Style, Image, TOC, Pt, ColorPalette, presets` exits 0
scenarios.scenario-2: presets registry contains exactly the agreed Phase-1 entries: 'alga_corporate', 'minimal', 'compact'; presets['alga_corporate'] is a path-or-dict resolvable by load_preset
scenarios.scenario-3: with_style_from(tmp_json) on a Document instance loads the preset and returns self for chaining
scenarios.scenario-4: SDK module re-exports do NOT shadow underlying modules — `mint_python.sdk.Document is mint_python.core.document.Document`

required-log-markers.marker-1: (none — SDK is a re-export module; downstream modules log)

forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-1: SDK must NOT export anything beyond the §3 surface — extras risk divergence from handover spec
forbidden-behaviors.forbidden-2: SDK must NOT import from src/mint/* (legacy) — the skeleton stays decoupled per V-MP-PKG forbidden-2

V-MP-SDK.autonomy-assessment: SWARM-SAFE — pure re-export + small registry; no I/O

Wave-1-1.goal: Foundation modules: config loads, sandbox executes valid code and blocks invalid

required-evidence.evidence-1: Config loads from env vars
required-evidence.evidence-2: Sandbox produces valid DOCX from hello_world fixture
required-evidence.evidence-3: Sandbox blocks fs and net access

Wave-1-2.goal: Full validation pipeline: rules → validate → fix → fingerprint

required-evidence.evidence-1: All D-H01..D-H10 rules detect violations in crafted fixtures
required-evidence.evidence-2: Validation report correctly classified by severity mode
required-evidence.evidence-3: Safe fixes applied, destructive rejected, cascade detected

Wave-1-3.goal: MCP G1 tools integration: validate + fix + fingerprint via MCP protocol

required-evidence.evidence-1: mint_validate returns structured violation report
required-evidence.evidence-2: mint_fix returns diff + backup path
required-evidence.evidence-3: mint_fingerprint returns hash

Wave-2-1.goal: Skill prompt selection + template engine basic fill

Wave-2-2.goal: Full generation pipeline: extract → create → validate → MCP tools

Wave-3-1.goal: QA pipeline (L1 programmatic + L2 structural ZIP analysis) and GRACE manifest injection ship together; existing Custom XML Parts from other vendors are preserved.

required-evidence.evidence-1: L1 programmatic check on minimal_valid_docx and minimal_valid_pptx returns confidence ≥ 0.95 within 1s; trace contains [QA][l1_check][BLOCK_L1_CHECK]
required-evidence.evidence-2: L2 structural path on minimal_valid_docx inspects OOXML ZIP structure and emits [QA][l2][BLOCK_L2_STRUCTURAL]. Gotenberg render/overlap evidence is not an executable Phase-3 gate in the current codebase.
required-evidence.evidence-3: GRACE bootstrap on docx_with_vendor_xml fixture preserves the non-GRACE Custom XML Parts byte-for-byte; trace contains [GRACE][inject][BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST] and [GRACE][preserve][BLOCK_PRESERVE_EXISTING]
required-evidence.evidence-4: GRACE manifest namespace urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest is present in the output and readable by a fresh OOXML parser

Wave-3-1.regression-confirmed: Empirically validated during the Phase-5 plan-audit run: full pytest suite (191 tests) passes, ruff clean, mypy clean. Commands and evidence-blocks reflect actual test behavior on the merged tree.

Wave-4-1.goal: Document planner: plan generation + plan validation

required-evidence.evidence-1: Plan validation accepts valid plans and rejects invalid ones
required-evidence.evidence-2: Plan prompt includes format-specific constraints (DXA, style IDs)
required-evidence.evidence-3: Plan generation produces structured JSON with sections, styles, numbering

Wave-4-2.goal: Section generator: per-section code generation + validation + retry

required-evidence.evidence-1: Section code validation catches imports, bracket errors, missing patterns
required-evidence.evidence-2: Section prompt includes plan context + sibling summaries
required-evidence.evidence-3: Retry logic handles failures and succeeds on retry

Wave-4-3.goal: Document assembler: template rendering + sandbox execution

required-evidence.evidence-1: Styles config renders correct JS for Heading1-6 with colors/sizes
required-evidence.evidence-2: Numbering config renders bullet + decimal levels
required-evidence.evidence-3: Full assembly produces valid DOCX passing L1 validation
required-evidence.evidence-4: Postprocessor applied: no empty comments, tblLayout fixed

Wave-4-4.goal: Full modular pipeline integration: plan → sections → assemble → validate

required-evidence.evidence-1: Modular create produces multi-section document with >10 paragraphs
required-evidence.evidence-2: MCP tools mint_plan, mint_generate_section, mint_assemble work independently
required-evidence.evidence-3: All VF-007 trace markers logged in correct sequence

Wave-5-1.goal: Pure-Python unpack/pack round-trip is tree-stable for XML parts and byte-stable for binary parts; auto-repair fires on the documented inputs.

required-evidence.evidence-1: unpack → pack → unpack round-trip on minimal_valid_docx, docx_with_grace, docx_with_vendor_xml: every XML part tree-equal, every binary part byte-equal, [Content_Types].xml override order preserved
required-evidence.evidence-2: Auto-repair on docx_with_durable_id_overflow regenerates the durableId; repaired_durable_ids ≥ 1 in PackResult
required-evidence.evidence-3: validate_relationships on docx_with_dangling_rel raises OOXML_RELATIONSHIP_BROKEN with the missing target named in the message
required-evidence.evidence-4: escape_smart_quotes on docx_with_quote_chars is idempotent: applying twice produces the same tree as applying once
required-evidence.evidence-5: No call site invokes LibreOffice / soffice / pandoc / any external binary. Static check: src/mint/ooxml.py imports only from {zipfile, lxml.etree, hashlib, pathlib, dataclasses, typing, logging, re, unicodedata}; the regex test asserts no occurrence of subprocess, os.system, os.popen, os.execv, os.execvp, pty.spawn, sh., or any 'shell=True' string literal. Positive framing: the only allowed external interaction is stdlib zipfile + lxml.

Wave-5-2.goal: Typed EditPlans applied deterministically; LLM never sees OOXML; revisions and comments follow ECMA-376 with reply linkage in commentsExtended.xml.

required-evidence.evidence-1: build_edit_prompt output contains no raw OOXML XML — assertion against the rendered prompt string using regex /<[a-z][a-z0-9]*:[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]*(\s|>|\/)/ which catches any opening tag with an XML namespace prefix (w:, w14:, w15:, w16:, wp:, wps:, a:, m:, mc:, r:, pic: and any future OOXML namespace). The same regex is the executable form of VF-010.forbidden-1.
required-evidence.evidence-2: tracked_replace + tracked_delete produce ECMA-376 w:ins / w:del with author and date; w:rPr preserved on both branches; full-paragraph delete also marks w:pPr/w:rPr/w:del
required-evidence.evidence-3: add_comment with parent_id=0 produces both top-level and reply entries in word/comments.xml plus a w15:commentEx paraIdParent record in word/commentsExtended.xml; commentRange markers are siblings of w:r
required-evidence.evidence-4: accept_change and reject_change behave per matrix in V-M-EDIT scenario-12 (insertion vs deletion)
required-evidence.evidence-5: Anchor resolution against ORIGINAL tree confirmed by VF-010.inv-4 + V-M-EDIT scenario-19 + scenario-21
required-evidence.evidence-6: EditPlan JSON uses 'type' as the discriminator on both EditOp and Anchor (V-M-EDIT scenario-23)
required-evidence.evidence-7: M-EDIT.edit on fixture-21 (docx_large_5mb, ~5MB, ~100 pages, ~100 paragraphs) with a 3-op EditPlan completes within p99 ≤ 5s wall-clock on the reference machine (CI runner spec, single-process, warm filesystem). Implementation note for the worker: M-OOXML.unpack/pack must avoid full reparse-on-every-part churn; M-EDIT must avoid O(n²) anchor scans. Latency budget is per-call, excluding LLM round-trip. Track regressions via pytest --benchmark or equivalent.

Wave-5-3.goal: End-to-end edit through the MCP tool and the CLI subcommand: EditPlan JSON in, EditResult JSON out, output document validated.

required-evidence.evidence-1: mint_edit MCP tool accepts edit_plan_json + author and returns EditResult JSON identical to direct M-EDIT.edit invocation
required-evidence.evidence-2: mint edit CLI subcommand reads EditPlan from a JSON file path and exits non-zero when EditResult.success is false
required-evidence.evidence-3: VF-010 trace-sequence emitted in correct order on a successful edit through the MCP path

Wave-6-1.SCOPE: M-CONFIG (engine field), M-CLI (--engine, _select_engine, BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE), MP-PKG, V-MP-FLAG
Wave-6-1.goal: Single-wave Phase-0 surface: dual-engine flag wired through M-CONFIG and M-CLI, MP-PKG skeleton importable, all V-MP-FLAG / V-MP-PKG scenarios green, every legacy DF-001..DF-007 trace sequence still passes when invoked under --engine js. Wave is intentionally small (one wave covers the whole Phase-6) because Phase 0 is scaffolding-only.

commands.command-4: ruff check src/mint/config.py src/mint/cli.py src/mint_python tests/unit/test_engine_flag.py tests/integration/test_cli.py

swarm-policy.isolation: Process-per-worker via pytest-xdist (-n auto) is recommended. In-process serial is acceptable ONLY if the clean_env autouse fixture in tests/unit/test_config.py has been extended to clear MINT_ENGINE; otherwise os.environ writes by main() leak between tests.
swarm-policy.writescope-controller: docs/development-plan.xml, docs/knowledge-graph.xml, docs/verification-plan.xml — workers MUST NOT touch.
swarm-policy.writescope-worker: src/mint/config.py, src/mint/cli.py, src/mint/__init__.py, src/mint_python/**, tests/unit/test_engine_flag.py, tests/integration/test_cli.py (only the _run helper), pyproject.toml ([tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel].packages line only), .env.example (one comment line only). Any other file is out-of-scope for this wave.

required-evidence.evidence-1: All V-MP-FLAG scenarios 1-11 pass (default→python, env override, garbage→CONFIG_INVALID, _select_engine branches, frozen invariant, arg position, CLI overrides env via os.environ pre-load, env isolation)
required-evidence.evidence-2: All V-MP-PKG scenarios 1-2, 4 pass at module level (import smoke, version, header convention)
required-evidence.evidence-3: V-M-CONFIG scenarios 1-7 all pass (existing 1-4 unchanged, new 5-7 cover MINT_ENGINE)
required-evidence.evidence-4: V-M-CLI scenarios 1-14 all pass; scenarios 1-7 succeed only after the test_cli `_run` helper is pinned to `--engine js`; scenarios 8-14 are new Phase-6 surface
required-evidence.evidence-5: VF-011 EngineDispatch trace-sequence holds on both branches; BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE marker present on every non-help main() call
required-evidence.evidence-6: VF-011 forbidden-signals 1-4 hold (no legacy markers on python path; no NotImplementedError on js path; no BLOCK_DISPATCH without preceding BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE; no double BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE)
required-evidence.evidence-7: VF-001..VF-006 trace sequences remain green when invoked via the CLI (`mint --engine js <subcmd>`); these are the flows that route through the chokepoint. VF-007..VF-010 are unaffected by Phase-6 by construction — their tests (test_mcp_g1.py, test_mcp_g2.py, test_create.py) use `from mint.mcp_g1 import ...` / `from mint.create import create` direct imports and bypass `_select_engine` entirely. See VF-011 chokepoint-scope.
required-evidence.evidence-8: `pyproject.toml` parses cleanly via `python -c "import tomllib; tomllib.load(open('pyproject.toml','rb'))"` AND `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel].packages` equals `["src/mint", "src/mint_python"]` exactly. Cheap module-level guard against typos before the phase-level `uv build` would catch them.
required-evidence.evidence-9: `.env.example` contains a commented-out `# MINT_ENGINE=js` line with an explainer comment immediately above it ("# Default is python; set js to keep the legacy Node.js sandbox path"). Greppable: `grep -E "^# *MINT_ENGINE=js" .env.example`.
required-evidence.evidence-10: USER-FACING COLLATERAL AUDIT (Phase-0 fail-closed default breaks bare `mint <subcmd>` examples until Phase 1 ships). Run a recursive grep for `mint ` invocations across: `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `docs/*.md`, `docs/pure-python/*.md`, `e2e_*.py` at repo root, any `*.sh` scripts, any `Makefile`/`justfile` targets. For EACH match: either (a) update to `mint --engine js <subcmd>` if the example must stay runnable today, OR (b) prefix with a clear comment "Phase-6 note: default MINT_ENGINE=python raises NotImplementedError until rollout Phase 1; set MINT_ENGINE=js or pass `--engine js` to run". Discovery command: `grep -rn -E "(^|[^a-z])(mint|uv run mint|python -m mint\.cli)\s+(serve|validate|fix|fingerprint|create|extract|edit)" README.md AGENTS.md docs/ e2e_*.py Makefile justfile *.sh scripts/*.sh 2>/dev/null`. The audit MUST be performed by the worker who flips the default in M-CONFIG; the worker's PR description lists every modified file. A worker who updates only code/tests but skips this audit fails Wave-6-1 acceptance.

swarm-policy.writescope-worker: src/mint_python/core/style.py, tests/unit/test_mp_style.py, docs/style-preset-schema.md, src/mint_python/core/presets/*.json (preset registry data).
